title: dianthera candelariae
intro: Dianthera candelariae, also known as Candle Bush or Dianthera, is a tropical plant species native to Central and South America, characterized by its clusters of small, white flowers. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Dianthera candelariae include its potential to reduce fever, relieve pain, and treat various infections. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and flowers are often made into teas, infusions, or tinctures to harness its medicinal properties.
